title: Florida: Digital Bill of Rights (FDBR) — limited applicability
summary: Florida's Digital Bill of Rights grants deletion and opt-out rights, but it applies only to very large companies (broadly, those with over $1 billion in global revenue meeting set criteria). Most data brokers and people-search sites fall outside it, so for removal you will usually rely on the broker's own opt-out rather than Florida law. The FDBR took effect on July 1, 2024.
disclaimer: General information, not legal advice, and not yet independently reviewed. Because the FDBR's reach is narrow, the broker's own opt-out (below) is usually the practical route — verify before relying on either.
